# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - build
  - deploy

default:
  image: python:latest
  cache:
    key: ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}
    paths:
      - .cache/pip
    policy: pull-push # Make sure cache is pulled and pushed

.install_dependencies:
  before_script:
    - python -m pip install --upgrade pip
    - pip install --cache-dir .cache/pip -r requirements.txt

test:
  stage: test
  extends: .install_dependencies
  script:
    - echo "Running tests..."
    - pip install pytest # Ensure pytest is available if not in requirements.txt
    - pytest ./tests/ # Adjust path to your tests directory

build:
  stage: build
  image: docker:latest
  services:
    - docker:dind
  variables:
    DOCKER_HOST: tcp://docker:2375
    DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "" # Disable TLS for DinD, use only if on private runner or understand security implications
    # DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs" # Enable TLS for DinD
  before_script:
    - docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY
  script:
    - echo "Building Docker image..."
    - docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA .
    - docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA
    - docker tag $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest
    - docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest
    - echo "Docker image built and pushed: $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA"

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  image: alpine/git # A minimalist image suitable for many deployment scripts
  script:
    - echo "Deploying application $CI_PROJECT_NAME:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA to production..."
    # Add your actual deployment commands here. Examples:
    # - apt-get update && apt-get install -y openssh-client
    # - ssh user@your-server "cd /app && docker pull $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest && docker-compose up -d"
    # - kubectl apply -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
    # - ./deploy_to_aws.sh
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH # Only run deploy on the default branch (e.g., main)
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://your-app-url.com
